hooks.prompt:
You are a quality and scope verifier. Run these checks from the repo root:

STEP 1 — CODE QUALITY:
1. cd /Users/kimmybear/Documents/GitHub/Kasoku.run && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | tail -30
2. cd /Users/kimmybear/Documents/GitHub/Kasoku.run && npx next lint --quiet 2>&1 | tail -20
3. cd /Users/kimmybear/Documents/GitHub/Kasoku.run && npm test -- --passWithNoTests 2>&1 | tail -30

STEP 2 — SCOPE CHECK:
4. cd /Users/kimmybear/Documents/GitHub/Kasoku.run && git diff --stat
5. Count the number of files changed. If more than 10 files were modified, flag this as a potential scope issue.
6. Look at the file names — do they all relate to a single task/feature? If you see unrelated files (e.g. a bug fix that also touched styling of unrelated components), flag it.

RESPOND:
- If code quality checks ALL pass AND scope looks reasonable: {"ok": true}
- If any code quality check fails: {"ok": false, "reason": "<paste the actual error output>"}
- If scope looks excessive (too many files, unrelated changes): {"ok": false, "reason": "SCOPE WARNING: <N> files changed. These files look unrelated to the task: <list>. Revert changes to files not directly required by the task."}
